articles.title: Bun 1.3: The Full-Stack JavaScript Runtime
articles.description: Arriving a few hours after last week’s issue (natch!) Bun 1.3 remains the big news of the past week. Bun is a performance and DX-focused JavaScriptCore-powered runtime which, with v1.3, balances being a drop-in Node.js replacement with becoming a ‘full-stack runtime’ among other exciting developments:
articles.author:
Full‑stack dev server with hot reloading built into Bun.serve

Improved GC with lower idle CPU and memory usage.
Built-in MySQL and Redis clients (alongside Postgres and SQLite).
The ability to bundle both frontend and backend apps into a single build.

Code signing for Windows and macOS executables you build.

Isolated pnpm-esque package installs are now the default.

articles.title: Announcing Vite+ / VitePlus
articles.description: Originally announced at last week's ViteConf, Evan shares more details about what this extended, unified Vite-based toolchain (now in ‘early access’) is, and the motivation behind it. Unlike regular Vite, it has a commercial angle but “will be free for individuals, open source projects, and small businesses” as well as “source available.”
articles.author: Evan You

articles.title: Node.js v25.0.0 (Current) Released
articles.description: The latest cutting edge version of Node has arrived with Web Storage enabled by default, JSON.stringify perf improvements, a new --allow-net option in the permission model, built-in Uint8Array base64/hex conversion, and WebAssembly and JIT optimizations.
articles.author: Rafael Gonzaga

articles.title: Improving the Trustworthiness of JavaScript on the Web
articles.description: A look at WAICT (Web Application Integrity, Consistency, and Transparency), an early-stage W3C-backed effort to bring stronger ‘app store-like’ security guarantees to the Web by ensuring the code a browser runs hasn’t been modified by attackers.
articles.author: Michael Rosenberg (Cloudflare)

articles.title: ▶  Beyond Signals
articles.description: A half-hour talk from the creator of SolidJS, exploring how signals (once pioneering, now mainstream) are shaping web development, their architectural implications, but why we’re only “at the beginning of the journey” and what comes next.
articles.author: Ryan Carniato

articles.title: Lazy Fields for 30x Speedup Without Decorators or Transforms
articles.description: Some clever work from the creators of the Joist ORM.
articles.author: Stephen Haberman (Joist)

articles.title: 🌿 A Quick Tutorial on the Barnsley Fern Fractal
articles.description: The Barnsley fern is a fractal that represents a plant-like fern structure.
articles.author: Slicker

articles.title: DOMPurify 3.3: A Fast, Tolerant XSS Sanitizer for HTML
articles.description: Supports all modern browsers (as well as Node.js) and is heavily tested. There’s a live demo here.
articles.author: Cure53

articles.title: python-node: Integrate Python ASGI Apps with Node.js
articles.description: Several months ago Platformatic unveiled php-node, a way to embed PHP directly within Node apps. Now they’re tackling Python with @platformatic/python-node, a Node-native bridge that embeds a Python interpreter and speaks ASGI.
articles.author: Stephen Belanger and Matteo Collina

articles.title: jsonriver: A Simple, Fast Streaming JSON Parser
articles.description: A library to parse JSON incrementally as it streams in, such as from a network request or an LLM, returning a sequence of ‘increasingly complete’ values.
articles.author: Peter Burns (Google)

articles.title: Kaluma 1.3: A Tiny JS Runtime for the Raspberry Pi Pico 2
articles.description: Can a JavaScript runtime squeeze onto an RP2350-based Raspberry Pi Pico 2? Kaluma can, all while offering some Node.js-like niceties. v1.3 is powered by the latest version of JerryScript, a lightweight JS engine for constrained environments.
articles.author: Kaluma Project

articles.title: Triplex: A Visual Workspace for Building React Components
articles.description: Originally a commercial tool for laying out components in 3D scenes rendered with React Three Fiber, Triplex is now open source. Its scope has increased with a VS Code extension and support for regular React DOM elements and components too.
articles.author: Mike Douges and Pmndrs

articles.description: Cloudflare Sandboxes is a new service for running untrusted JavaScript (and Python) code in a safe container-based 'sandbox' environment.
The Remix project's Remix Jam event took place last week, and you can now ▶️ watch the full livestream. If you want to see the reveal and demo of Remix 3, jump to 03:24:30 in the video.
Why is typeof null === 'object'? Piotr Zarycki goes much deeper into the history than you might expect.
The Lit project is joining the OpenJS Foundation.
React Native 0.82 – It's a 'new era' as React Native now runs entirely on the New Architecture.
Graffle 7.3 – GraphQL client library formerly known as graphql-request. v7.3 adds CommonJS support for compatibility with Jest and non-ESM systems.
Skeleton v4.0 – Adaptive design system powered by Tailwind CSS. See the homepage for a preview and more.

articles.title: Firefox 144 Released: All Major Browsers Now Support View Transitions
articles.description: A notable release as the View Transitions API is finally supported (as in all other major browsers). Other features include support for the moveBefore() method, profile management, changes to Picture-in-Picture, a new visual search option, and more. Here are the developer release notes.
articles.author: Firefox

articles.title: CSS Grid: A Helpful Mental Model and the Power of Grid Lines
articles.description: Saron shares a mental model for helping us best understand CSS Grid, attempting to demystify things via a container-first approach. There are snippets and examples to illustrate just how grid lines can help with precise element placement.
articles.author: Saron Yitbarek (WebKit)

articles.title: 🔢 Interop Feature Ranking
articles.description: A new site from the Firefox team for sharing which of the future Interop features you care about most. Log in with GitHub then drag the various web platform features around to your liking to influence their future development.
articles.author: Firefox

articles.title: Modern CSS Round-Out Tabs
articles.description: Chris revisits an old tabular user interface pattern (as seen above) and updates it with modern CSS thanks to the shape() feature.
articles.author: Chris Coyier

articles.title: The Thing About contrast-color
articles.description: The current contrast-color() function chooses either white for dark backgrounds or black for light ones, which, despite the intention, may not make for the most comfortable reading. Geoff at CSS-Tricks shared his thoughts on this too.
articles.author: Andy Clarke

articles.title: The New progress() Function in CSS
articles.description: Shares some solid possible scenarios where you may want to use this handy function.
articles.author: Amit Merchant

articles.title: CSS :is() :where() the Magic Happens
articles.description: Revisits these two pseudo-class functions, looking at some use-cases, and how they can now be combined with :has().
articles.author: Matthias Ott

articles.title: The Killer Feature of Web Components
articles.description: Dave sings the praises of Custom Elements Manifest (CEM), a feature he notes as being somewhat overlooked, but one that’s a powerful community-driven JSON format for surfacing all sorts of information about your component APIs.
articles.author: Dave Rupert

articles.title: Mirrow: A DSL for Creating and Animating SVGs
articles.description: The idea is that instead of SVGs being static assets, you can make them ‘living’ documents by providing a more code-like format for defining them. Check out the playground to interact with some Mirror code directly and see what you can create.
articles.author: Mirrow Team

articles.title: Triplex: A Visual Workspace for Building React Three Fiber Components
articles.description: Originally a commercial tool for visually laying out components in 3D scenes rendered with React Three Fiber, Triplex has gone open source and joined the Poimandres Collective (responsible for projects like Three Fiber, Jotai, and Zustand). Its scope has also increased with a VS Code extension and support for regular React DOM elements and components.
articles.author: Mike Douges and Pmndrs

articles.title: ogImage.click: A Free Open Graph Image Generator
articles.description: Choose from different templates for the platform you want the image to display on. You can also use an API to generate the images on the fly via JSON or cURL.
articles.author: Fady Mak

articles.title: Dev Tools: 20+ Privacy-First Productivity Tools for Developers
articles.description: Similar to other such resources. This one uniquely features keyboard shortcuts to switch tools/pages and includes tools for text, encoding/conversion, and generators, among others.
articles.author: Hitarth shah

articles.title: 📊 Make Graph: Create Beautiful Charts and Graphs Online
articles.description: A nicely designed tool with 10 chart types (bar, line, pie, scatter, heatmap, etc.), 10+ themes, prebuilt templates, and features for making the charts more accessible with visual pattern textures.
articles.author: Make Graph

articles.title: 📄 EmbedPDF: A PDF Viewer That Integrates with Any JavaScript Project
articles.description: No dependencies, supports any framework, and has features like themes, annotations, search, smooth scrolling, etc. Docs and a live demo here.
articles.author: EmbedPDF

articles.title: Bun 1.3: The Alternative Full-Stack JS Runtime
articles.description: The performance-focused JavaScriptCore-powered runtime gets a big upgrade making it more of a ‘full-stack runtime’ including a dev server with hot reloading (yes, you can 'run' HTML files with Bun now — as above), the ability to scaffold React projects (with Tailwind and Shadcn!) out of the box, and isolated package installs by default. There’s a lot to like in here for front-end developers.
articles.author: The Bun Team
